_meta.purpose: PAPER TEST ONLY (Stage 3x.3 portability validation). Not a real client. Drafted against the wolfhouse-somo.baseline.json schema to surface where the engine is still surf-house-shaped. This is NOT used at runtime. See docs/ROADMAP.md § Engine portability.

vertical_profile._note: Vertical-specific shape. The shared spine (payment/confirmation/handoff/llm_safety/privacy) is reused unchanged; only this block + catalog + inventory differ.

inventory._note: Replaces the lodging beds/rooms model. Provider type = 'rentals'. Unit = item with size variants; availability = overlapping time windows minus reserved quantity.

catalog._note: Replaces 'packages'. Generic priced offerings. No surf-camp package names; pricing is per item per window.

required_fields._note: Vertical override of the spine's required-field map. Note: NO dates-as-nights, NO guest_count-as-beds — this is where lodging assumptions leak.

payment._note: Reused spine section, unchanged shape. Deposit vs full-upfront unknown for this demo.
payment._security_deposit_note: RENTAL-SPECIFIC: damage/security hold has no equivalent in the lodging baseline schema — see _portability_findings.

confirmation._note: Reused spine section. Confirmation should include pickup time/location instead of check-in/checkout.

handoff._note: Reused spine section, unchanged across verticals.
handoff.handoff_safe_reply: Let me connect you with our team — they'll be with you shortly.

llm_safety._note: Reused spine section verbatim. Not vertical-specific.

customer_memory_privacy._note: Reused spine section verbatim. Not vertical-specific.

_portability_findings._note: OUTPUT of the paper test — these are the gaps where wolfhouse-somo.baseline.json assumes a surf house. Each becomes a Stage 5 extraction backlog item so the spine is vertical-neutral.
required_schema_generalizations: Top-level 'packages' must become generic 'catalog.offerings' (package names are surf-camp-specific).
'rooming' block must be optional/capability-gated — absent entirely for rentals.
Availability model must be abstracted: lodging uses date-range bed overlap; rentals use item x time-window x quantity x size. Needs InventoryProvider interface.
required_fields keyed on (check_in/check_out nights, guest_count->beds) leak lodging assumptions; must be per-vertical required-field map.
deposit_scope options are lodging-flavored (per_person/per_package); rentals need per_item/full_upfront and a SECURITY/DAMAGE hold concept that does not exist in baseline.
confirmation include_* fields assume check-in/checkout + house rules; rentals need pickup/return time+location + damage policy.
'guest' vocabulary vs 'customer/renter' — keep spine vocabulary neutral or config-driven.
_portability_findings.verdict: Backbone is ~70% reusable as-is (entire payment/confirmation/handoff/safety spine). The non-reusable 30% is concentrated exactly at the catalog + inventory/availability seam — confirming the ROADMAP portability plan. No spine rewrite needed; build InventoryProvider + generic catalog and gate rooming.
